title: Dynamical Threshold Theory of Sleep
classification_ja: 睡眠相転移・回復ダイナミクスのネットワーク理論
central_claim: mathematical network prototype for sleep-like recovery phase transitions, not clinical or empirical validation

prediction_summary.paper_result: spatially heterogeneous accumulation of homeostatic burden
rapid growth of mean sleep propensity into a sleep-dominant regime
bounded global downscaling of mean coupling strength toward finite fixed points
reduced free energy relaxes after early transient overshoot toward a bounded plateau
edges with stronger pre-sleep protection are preferentially preserved during sleep

claim_boundary: No clinical sleep diagnosis, medical advice, human-data validation, therapeutic claim, or complete biological sleep theory claim.
